meta.description: Coaching tips for Level 3-4: Prompt Engineering + Context Management

tips.tip: Structure your prompts with Context → Requirements → Acceptance criteria → Tech constraints → Examples (CRATE).
tips.example_after: Context: We have a product list in src/products/. Requirements: Add search by name and category. Acceptance: Results update as user types, <200ms response. Tech: Use existing Fuse.js dependency.

tips.tip: Always include constraints in your prompt — file size limits, performance targets, existing patterns to follow.
tips.example_after: Create a data table component. Constraints: Must handle 10k+ rows (use virtualization), follow existing TableBase pattern in src/components/, support column sorting.

tech_variants.react: Follow existing TableBase pattern in src/components/, use react-window for virtualization.
tech_variants.vue: Follow existing TableBase pattern in src/components/, use vue-virtual-scroller.
tech_variants.python: Follow existing BaseModel pattern, support pagination for 10k+ records, use SQLAlchemy lazy loading.

tips.tip: Aim for one-shot success: if AI needs >3 rounds to get it right, your prompt probably needs more context or clearer acceptance criteria.
tips.example_after: Before starting: specify file paths, error handling strategy, test expectations, and edge cases upfront.

tips.tip: Create a CLAUDE.md in your project root with your tech stack, coding conventions, and directory structure.
tips.example_before: [No CLAUDE.md, AI guesses your project structure]
tips.example_after:
# CLAUDE.md
## Tech Stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Prisma
## Conventions: Use server components by default
## Structure: src/app/ (routes), src/lib/ (utilities)

tech_variants.python:
# CLAUDE.md
## Tech Stack: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy
## Conventions: Type hints required, async endpoints
## Structure: src/api/ (routes), src/models/ (DB models)
tech_variants.go:
# CLAUDE.md
## Tech Stack: Go 1.22, Chi router, GORM
## Conventions: Table-driven tests, context propagation
## Structure: cmd/ (entrypoints), internal/ (business logic)

tips.tip: Update your CLAUDE.md when you add new patterns or change conventions — stale instructions cause AI to generate outdated code.
tips.example_before: CLAUDE.md says 'use REST' but project migrated to GraphQL last month
tips.example_after: After migration: update CLAUDE.md to reflect GraphQL patterns, remove REST references, add schema location.

tips.tip: For tasks touching 3+ files, use Plan Mode first — let AI create a plan, review it, then execute.
tips.example_before: [Asks AI to implement directly, ends up with scattered changes across 8 files]
tips.example_after: Start with: 'Plan how to add user authentication — list all files that need changes and the order of changes.'

tips.tip: When reviewing AI's plan, check: Are all affected files listed? Is the change order correct? Any missing edge cases?
tips.example_after: The plan misses updating the API route types. Also, step 3 should come before step 2 because the model depends on the schema.

tips.tip: If a plan has >10 steps, it's too big. Break the feature into smaller, independently testable chunks.
tips.example_before: Plan: 15 steps to add full auth system
tips.example_after: Chunk 1: User model + migration (3 steps). Chunk 2: Login endpoint + tests (4 steps). Chunk 3: Session middleware (3 steps).

tips.tip: Learn the key Claude Code commands: /init (setup project), /compact (reduce context), /cost (check usage).
tips.example_after: Start each session with /init in a new project. Use /compact when context gets long. Check /cost periodically.
